title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: A timeless masterpiece that delves into the decadence and disillusionment of the Jazz Age, masking a pursuit of lost love.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: A profound novel exploring themes of wealth, love, and the American Dream through a vivid portrayal of the East and West Egg.

author: George Orwell
description: A dystopian exploration of societal excess and the human condition, depicting a haunting narrative that transcends its era.

title: The Catcher in the Rye
author: J.D. Salinger
description: An enigmatic story exploring themes of identity and the struggles of young adulthood in a changing world.

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: A satirical romance that remains a literary gem, showcasing the glittering yet elusive world of 19th-century society.

title: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: An epic adventure set in a fantasy world, weaving a compelling narrative of courage and discovery.

title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
author: J.K. Rowling
description: The story of a young wizard's journey into magic, friendship, and his fight against dark forces.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A philosophical adventure about following your dreams and finding your own personal legend.

title: The Girl on the Train
author: Paula Hawkins
description: A gripping psychological suspense novel that keeps readers on the edge with its mysterious twists.
